name: Test generation
description: Generate unit, integration, or property-based tests from specifications or code.

placeholders.name: test_framework
placeholders.description: Framework and runner (e.g. pytest, Jest, go test, JUnit 5).

placeholders.name: code_under_test
placeholders.description: Implementation to cover, including signatures and important branches.

placeholders.name: requirements
placeholders.description: Behaviors, edge cases, invariants, or acceptance criteria to assert.

placeholders.name: style_notes
placeholders.description: Naming conventions, fixture patterns, mocking libraries, or files to mirror.

expected_output_format.description: Emit complete, runnable test code in the requested language; include imports and minimal fixtures only where needed.
sections: Brief plan (bullet list) of scenarios covered
Full test file(s) in fenced code blocks
Notes on any required test doubles or environment setup

examples.title: Pure function edge cases

placeholder_values.requirements: Happy path; value below low; value above high; equal to bounds; error when low > high; error message text.
placeholder_values.style_notes: Use `@pytest.mark.parametrize`; no external mocks; one test module `test_clamp.py` style.

examples.sample_response:
## Plan
- Parametrize interior and boundary values.
- Separate test for invalid range raising `ValueError` with message check.

```python
import pytest
from mymodule import clamp


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "value,low,high,expected",
    [
        (5, 0, 10, 5),
        (-1, 0, 10, 0),
        (99, 0, 10, 10),
        (0, 0, 10, 0),
        (10, 0, 10, 10),
    ],
)
def test_clamp_happy_and_boundaries(value, low, high, expected):
    assert clamp(value, low, high) == expected


def test_clamp_rejects_inverted_range():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="low must be"):
        clamp(3, high=1, low=5)
```

## Notes
No extra setup; import path assumes `mymodule` is on `PYTHONPATH`.
